summary: World-first formal consensus protocol for federation-wide constitutional amendments in a constitutionally governed autonomous AI system. FGCON-0 enforces strict majority quorum (floor(N/2)+1) before any federation-level invariant can be ratified — no single instance can unilaterally amend federation governance. FGCON-UNILATERAL-0 blocks duplicate votes per instance. FGCON-CHAIN-0 provides a tamper-evident HMAC-SHA256 chained audit ledger of every vote. FGCON-GATE-0 requires HUMAN-0 acknowledgement before ratification. FGCON-DETERM-0 guarantees deterministic proposal and record IDs from epoch_id + instance_id inputs only.
ip_claims: Strict majority quorum consensus protocol for federation-wide constitutional amendments with fail-closed default and HUMAN-0 gate enforcement
Per-instance unilateral vote prevention (FGCON-UNILATERAL-0) in a constitutionally governed multi-instance AI federation
HMAC-SHA256 chain-linked append-only ledger for cryptographic auditability of distributed governance consensus events
world_first_claim: First formal consensus protocol for federation-wide constitutional amendments in an autonomous AI code governance system, with strict majority quorum enforcement, per-instance anti-unilateral controls, and cryptographic chain-linked vote audit ledger
extends: INNOV-33 KBEP (federation knowledge exchange → federation governance voting)
